title: 2013 Cape Horn 23’ w/ Yamaha 250HP
description: Great condition 2013 The Yamaha LF250XCA is a 250HP, V6 4.2L with only 2000 hours counter-rotating four-stroke outboard that has been in production from at least 2011 through 2021 and later. It is not limited to a single year but represents a model series (V6 4.2L) Trolling motor All new five batteries Huge cooler. Live bait well. Audio system The 22’ hull is 1999 in great shape old school quality . Produced in Pensacola Florida. Trailer worthy with absolutely new tires. Ready to fishing The 1999 Cape Horn 22 Center Console (CC) is a rugged, unsinkable offshore fishing boat. Known for a heavy-duty, wood-free fiberglass construction and a wide beam, it handles rough water exceptionally well. Used models typically sell between \(\$ \) and \(\$ \), depending on engine condition.Key SpecificationsLength Overall: 22 feet (typically 20'8" to 22' depending on pulpit/bracket)Beam: 8 feet 6 inchesFuel Capacity: ~128 gallonsHull Construction: 12 layers of hand-laid glass, foam-filled, completely wood-freeMaximum Horsepower: Up to ~300 HP (commonly rigged with 200–250 HP outboard engines)Fishing & Design FeaturesUnsinkable Design: Features a foam-filled hull and high freeboard, making it highly regarded for safety in offshore conditions.Layout: Built as a no-nonsense fishing machine. It lacks a traditional molded inner liner in favor of raw, hose-and-go simplicity.Storage & Amenities: Includes a massive locking center console, a large bow fish box, and a spacious circular transom livewell.Market & ValuationBecause of its hardcore offshore reputation, these boats hold their value for anglers who want a durable, rough-water hull. Vintage values and specs can be researched directly on the JD Power database.If you are looking to purchase one or want to compare current listings, you can browse available inventory on Boats.com. To get a deeper sense of owner sentiment regarding its build and "unsinkable" claims, check out the continuousWave discussion forums. For more details on the brand's current manufacturing philosophy, visit Cape Horn

title: 2005 Mako 212 Central Console w/225 Honda
description: The 2005 Mako 212 Center Console paired with a Honda 225 hp four-stroke outboard is a rugged, deep-V offshore fishing rig. With a solid 8.5-foot beam and 93-gallon fuel capacity, it offers 360-degree fishability and a smooth ride in choppy waters.Key Specs & FeaturesLength: 21' 5"Beam: 8' 6"Dry Weight (Hull): ~3,000 lbsMax HP: 250 hpFuel Capacity: 93 gallonsDeadrise: 20 degreesStandard Features: Built-in insulated fish boxes, leaning post with rod holders, raw water washdown, and a console large enough for a porta-potty or dry storage.Honda 225 PerformanceThe Honda BF225 is a popular pairing for this hull, providing reliable VTEC technology for hole-shot acceleration and strong mid-range torque. Cruising speeds typically sit in the 28 to 33 mph range (yielding ~3.5 MPG), with a wide-open throttle (WOT) reaching roughly 45 to 48 mph depending on load and prop selection.Market & PricingOn the used market, a 2005 Mako 212 with a Honda 225 usually lists between $ and $ , heavily dependent on engine hours, the condition of the upholstery, and whether a trailer or updated electronics are included
